meta.title: European documentary series — Collision Productions
meta.description: Collision Productions, the studio behind Génération Do It Yourself — one of France's most-listened business podcasts — is producing a European documentary series for 2026-2027. It films those thinking and building the Europe of tomorrow.

hero.titlePost: before it stops telling its own story.
hero.sub1: Collision Productions is the studio behind Génération Do It Yourself — one of France's most-listened business podcasts: 100M+ cumulative listens, 1.5M per month, around 70% completion rate. Four long-form documentary series have followed, on China, the United States, Brazil and the GR20.
hero.sub2: Our next series films Europe. Not its announced decline, not the commentary already being written on every panel — but the founders, engineers, scientists and leaders who, across the continent, refuse the slide and are building something else.

paragraphs: Europe is entering the decade that will decide its standing. The dominant commentary tells a story of accepted decline: industrial slippage, political fracture, shrinking demographics, democratic disillusion. That frame is written every morning in the editorial pages. It is not wrong. It is not enough.

Another Europe exists, quieter and more stubborn. Founders who are reindustrialising, researchers who stay, leaders who invest here, creators who choose this continent as their ground. They do not hold the podium and do not sign op-eds. Our job is to put them on record.

Collision Productions grew out of Génération Do It Yourself, which became, in a few years, one of France's most-listened business podcasts: 100M+ cumulative listens, 1.5M a month, an average completion rate of around 70%. Four long-form documentary series — China, the United States, Brazil, the GR20 — extended that work into places that have to be visited to be understood.

This European series follows the same method: a long, dense interview, filmed at the guest's home or on their ground, edited with care, distributed on the platforms where our audience already lives. An archive of the moment, told by those who are building it. Production runs from late 2026 into 2027, ahead of the European election that will freeze every narrative.

blocks.label: Shooting location
blocks.value: At the guest's home or on their ground

blocks.label: Language
blocks.value: French, English or the guest's own language, subtitled

blocks.label: Review rights
blocks.value: Editorial review, no veto on cuts

method.schedule: Shooting from autumn 2026 to spring 2027. Rolling release from summer 2027.

guestsConfirmed.fallbackPartners: __TODO__ Partner organisation logos displayed in the meantime.

columns.title: An audience that decides
columns.body: Our audience — 1.5 million listeners a month, decision makers, founders, executives, cognitive elites across France and increasingly across Europe — does not consume this content passively. They quote it, comment it, watch it through. Average completion rate around 70%.

columns.title: A long archive
columns.body: Full unedited interviews are preserved and remain available to the guest, for life. You walk away with a dense editorial document — not a clip cut for social feeds.

columns.title: A collective narrative
columns.body: You contribute to a civic European positioning, the opposite of any vanity-media logic. The series places each interview inside a larger whole, and each guest inside a conversation other guests will keep building.

meta.title: Editorial partnership — European series — Collision Productions
meta.description: Collision Productions, publisher of Génération Do It Yourself and of four long-form documentary series, is opening partnerships on its next European series, 2026-2027. Audience: decision makers, founders, cognitive elites.

hero.sub: Collision Productions is the publisher of Génération Do It Yourself — one of France's most-listened business podcasts (100M+ cumulative listens, 1.5M per month, around 70% completion) — and of four long-form documentary series on China, the United States, Brazil and the GR20. Our next series films Europe. You associate with a European media asset — not an ad inventory — in front of an audience of decision makers, founders and cognitive elites, France first, then EU.

stats.note: Figures consolidated across all Collision productions.

paragraphs: Europe is entering the decade that will decide its standing. The dominant commentary tells a story of accepted decline. That frame is not wrong; it is not enough. Another Europe exists — founders reindustrialising, researchers staying, leaders investing here — that no major media is filming with the time it deserves.

This series will be an editorial asset that travels for years, on the platforms where our audience already chooses what it reads. You are not buying a placement — you are sitting alongside a collective narrative that European decision makers will quote, comment and archive.

The window is narrow. Before the 2027 European election, few productions will have had the time to film this moment from the inside. Brands that partner now capture the value: a narrative that will outlast them, and that no one can shoot again in the same place.

items.name: Title partner
items.description: Exclusive series naming, generic presence across every episode, possible editorial integration on relevant topics, preview access, long-term content rights.

items.name: Embedded partner
items.description: Episode-by-episode integration, targeted on a relevant theme. Systematic mention in credits and associated posts. Short-term rights.

items.name: Product partner
items.description: Goods or services exchange for shoots: travel, accommodation, equipment, post-production. No cash, equivalent editorial value.

contact.body: Direct conversation with the partnership team. Reply within 72 hours. Short first call, no commitment, to align on the format before the deck.
contact.calendlyLabel: Book a call with Christofer or Matthieu

form.consent: By submitting this form, you agree to be contacted by the partnerships team.
